title: Disciplinary and separation records for Sgt. Kelly Sargent – UNT Police Department

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records relating to any internal affairs investigations, administrative investigations, or misconduct investigations involving Sgt. Kelly Sargent (sometimes listed as “Sergeant Kelly Sargent” or “Sgt. Sargent”) of the University of North Texas Police Department, from January 1, 2016 to December 31, 2018. This includes, but is not limited to, complaints, investigative reports, witness statements, findings, recommendations, disciplinary letters, notices of suspension, and notices of termination.
All records showing any disciplinary action, including written reprimands, suspensions, demotions, or termination, taken against Sgt. Kelly Sargent during the same period.
Records reflecting any change in employment status for Sgt. Kelly Sargent during the same period, including but not limited to resignation letters, separation notices, last day of employment, settlement or separation agreements, and any documents stating the reasons for his separation from employment.
Any correspondence (emails or letters) between UNT Police Department command staff or Human Resources and Sgt. Kelly Sargent that discusses discipline, performance issues, or separation from employment during the same period, to the extent such records are not exempt from disclosure.
If you contend that any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, please produce all reasonably segregable non‑exempt portions and provide a written explanation citing the specific sections of Texas law you rely on to withhold or redact any material.
I am not requesting Social Security numbers, home addresses, dates of birth, or other information that is specifically exempt by law, and I agree that such personal identifiers may be redacted.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Drew Bee

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records concerning below:
Name: John Cupp
Branch: United States Army
DOB: February 1, 1951
Dates of Service: 1971 to 1984

Please provide the following military service information:
1. dates of service, dates of ranks, stations, list of awards, unit decorations
2. Section VII CURRENT AND PREVIOUS ASSIGNMENTS form
3. hard copy of Bronze Star Medal award citation
4. Military Education/ training courses
5. Section V MISCELLANEOUS Form
6. hard copy of Army commendation award citation

Historical research.

First name: John
Middle Initial: C
Last name: Cupp

I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information. Redact per U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).
Please mail hardcopies to:

MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley
